subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story and plot of the poem
Identify main characters and their actions
Recognize the moral of the legend
Develop reading comprehension and basic analysis skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
topics_covered: Poet’s style and approach to moral stories
Overview of poem’s theme: selfishness and consequences
teaching_strategy: Discussion about folklore and its importance
student_activities: Note down the main idea of the story

lesson_plan.title: Character Identification
teaching_strategy: Explain the role of each character
Ask students to describe actions in simple words
student_activities: Write names and traits of main characters
Share orally one action of each character

lesson_plan.title: Plot and Events
topics_covered: Sequence of events in the legend
Cause and effect: selfish action leads to consequence
teaching_strategy: Break down stanzas into simple steps
Ask students to repeat the sequence
student_activities: Create a simple flowchart of events
Retell the story in own words

lesson_plan.title: Moral of the Poem
topics_covered: Lesson about selfishness and its consequences
Relevance of moral to students’ daily life
teaching_strategy: Teacher explains moral in simple language
Students relate it to real-life examples
student_activities: Write one sentence on moral learned

teaching_aids: Flowchart or chart paper for story events
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple, focus on the story and moral, and encourage students to relate it to their own experiences.
